label: Keyword Strategy
description: Topic-cluster keyword strategy mapped to intent and funnel

fields.label: What you offer & goals
fields.placeholder: B2B CRM for small agencies. Goal: organic signups, not just traffic.

fields.label: Seed topics / products
fields.placeholder: client management, agency invoicing, project tracking, CRM alternatives

label: SEO Content Brief
description: Writer-ready brief to rank for a target keyword

fields.label: Page goal & context
fields.placeholder: Rank top 3 and drive trial signups. We sell an agency CRM.

fields.label: Secondary / semantic keywords
fields.placeholder: agency client management software, crm for marketing agencies, ...

label: On-Page SEO Audit
description: Page-level audit of content and on-page signals

fields.label: Current page content / structure
fields.placeholder: Paste the copy, headings, title tag, meta description, and current ranking if known.

fields.label: Top-ranking competitors
fields.placeholder: URLs currently ranking 1-3 for the keyword, with anything notable about them.

template:
You are a senior on-page SEO specialist auditing the page "{{pageUrl}}" targeting "{{targetKeyword}}".

## Page under review
{{pageContent}}
{{#if competitors}}
## Competitors ranking above
{{competitors}}
{{/if}}
## Audit
- Assess these dimensions: {{checks}}.
- For each issue: state the problem, why it costs rankings for this keyword, and the specific fix (concrete, not generic).
- Compare against the intent and, where given, the ranking competitors to find content/topical gaps.
{{#if rewrite}}- Provide a rewritten meta title (<=60 chars), meta description (<=155 chars), and an improved H1.
{{/if}}- Recommend structural changes (headings to add/merge), entities to add, and internal links.

## Deliverables
1. A prioritized issue list (issue, impact, fix, effort).
2. The optimized content outline / heading structure.
{{#if rewrite}}3. The rewritten metadata block ready to ship.
{{/if}}
Work only from the content provided; where data (e.g. current ranking) is missing, note it and proceed with reasoned recommendations.

label: Technical SEO Audit
description: Crawl, indexation and Core Web Vitals audit plan

fields.label: Stack, scale & known issues
fields.placeholder: Next.js SSR, ~40k product pages, faceted navigation, recent traffic drop after migration.

template:
You are a technical SEO lead auditing "{{siteName}}" ({{platform}} rendering, {{scale}} site).

## Context
{{siteContext}}

- Audit areas: {{areas}}
{{#if migration}}- Treat this as a post-migration audit: prioritize regressions, redirects, and lost equity.
{{/if}}
## Approach
- For each area, list what to check, the symptom of failure, how to detect it, and the remediation, tailored to {{platform}} rendering at {{scale}} scale.
- Distinguish indexation/crawl-budget concerns appropriate to the site size; for large sites prioritize crawl efficiency and faceted-URL control.
- Flag rendering pitfalls specific to the platform (e.g. hydration, JS-dependent content, CDN caching).
{{#if tooling}}- Recommend tools and concrete checks/queries (log-file analysis, GSC reports, crawler config) to validate each finding.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. A prioritized findings table (issue, area, severity, impact on rankings, fix, owner).
2. A crawl/indexation health summary and Core Web Vitals action list.
3. A sequenced remediation roadmap (this week / this quarter).

Use standard technical-SEO best practice for anything unspecified and call out checks that need live data access.

label: Backlink Strategy
description: White-hat link-building plan with outreach angles

fields.label: Niche, assets & link goals
fields.placeholder: Product-analytics SaaS. Have a free benchmark report and an open-source SDK. Want authority + referral traffic.

label: Meta Tags & SERP Snippet
description: Optimized titles, descriptions and structured data

fields.label: Page content & value prop
fields.placeholder: Transparent per-seat pricing, free trial, no setup fee. Differentiator: agency-specific features.

label: SERP Competitor Analysis
description: Reverse-engineer the SERP and find the winning angle

fields.label: Your offering & objective
fields.placeholder: We're a PM tool for creative teams. Want to break into the top 5 for this term.

fields.label: Current top results
fields.placeholder: 1) g2.com listicle, 2) competitor A guide, 3) Forbes Advisor, 4) Reddit thread, ...

fields.label: Output a content blueprint to win

template:
You are a competitive SEO analyst reverse-engineering the SERP for "{{keyword}}".

## Objective
{{goal}}

- Your relative authority: {{domainStrength}}
- Analyze along: {{dimensions}}

## Current top results
{{competitors}}

## Analysis
- Determine the dominant intent and the content format Google is rewarding for this query.
- For each competing result, summarize its angle, depth, structure, and apparent authority, and why it ranks.
- Identify shared patterns (the table-stakes a page must have) and, crucially, the gaps and weaknesses no one is covering well.
- Given a {{domainStrength}} domain, recommend a realistic differentiation strategy (out-depth, out-format, unique data, or niche-down) rather than a like-for-like copy.
{{#if blueprint}}- Produce a content blueprint: recommended format, H2/H3 outline, must-cover entities, SERP features to capture, and the unique angle.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. A SERP intent + format verdict.
2. A competitor comparison table (page, angle, strengths, weaknesses).
3. The differentiation strategy{{#if blueprint}} and the content blueprint to execute it{{/if}}.

Reason from the results provided; where signals (e.g. backlink counts) are unknown, infer cautiously and label inferences.
